text.title: Tokio async: cancellation safety, Mutex rules, and agent gotchas
text.summary: Cancellation-safety rules, std::sync::Mutex-in-async antipatterns, and the most common LLM mistakes when generating Tokio code.

rule.statement: When using tokio::select! with non-cancellation-safe futures (e.g., AsyncReadExt::read_to_end, AsyncWriteExt::write_all), document the cancel-safety status of each branch with a comment. Cancelling a non-safe future at an arbitrary .await point drops partial state silently.
rule.rationale: tokio::select! drops the losing future at the next .await point. If the future has partially written state (a half-flushed buffer, an uncommitted transaction), the partial state is silently lost with no error.

rule.statement: Never hold std::sync::MutexGuard across a .await point. Use tokio::sync::Mutex only when the critical section itself must .await. For most cases, prefer message-passing (tokio::sync::mpsc) over shared mutable state.
rule.rationale: std::sync::MutexGuard is not Send. Holding it across .await causes a compile error if the type is caught, or a deadlock if the executor tries to move the future to another thread. The compiler does not always catch this; clippy::await_holding_lock does.

antipattern.description: Wrapping sqlx::PgPool or reqwest::Client in Mutex<T>.
antipattern.reason: Both types are already Send + Sync (they use internal Arc-wrapped pools). Adding a Mutex serializes all access, defeating the pool's purpose.

antipattern.description: tokio::spawn with a closure that captures &Database or any non-'static reference.
antipattern.reason: tokio::spawn requires Send + 'static. Agents fix this by adding Arc::new everywhere; review for over-Arc'd state that should be owned or passed by value.
Blocks the Tokio executor thread, starving all other tasks on that thread. Strictly forbidden inside async code. Use .await instead.
JoinHandle returns Result with JoinError on panic. Discarding it swallows panics silently. Use .await?? or explicitly match the JoinError.

antipattern.description: Mixing async-std::task::spawn and tokio::spawn in the same project.
antipattern.reason: Different runtimes; futures from one cannot be awaited by the other. Pin Tokio as the single runtime in the system prompt.
